title: South Korea
dek: Eight days based in Seoul — palaces, markets, gaming districts and street food — with a weekend in Daejeon for MSI 2026. Everything below is checked; tick items off as you go and your progress saves.
footer: Location photos via Wikimedia Commons — tap any credit for its source, author and license.
intro: Seoul base, with an overnight trip to Daejeon for the MSI 2026 weekend (Jul 11–12).

sections.title: When you land
sections.body: You land at Terminal 1, Incheon Airport (~05:30–06:30 KST on Thursday Jul 9). Air Premia YP132 arrives at T1 — the AREX station is inside the terminal, no shuttle needed. Budget 30–60 min through immigration at this hour. In order:
checklist: Scan your e-Arrival Card QR at the automated immigration gate (saved offline on your phone — done Jul 7)
Pick up a T-money card at any convenience store or ticket machine in arrivals; load ≈₩20,000
Take the AREX All-Stop from T1 to Seoul Station (≈59 min, ≈₩5,350 — trains every 5–15 min from 05:14 onward; Express saves 15 min but costs ≈₩13,000 and needs a reservation — not worth it at 06:00)
At Seoul Station, take Line 4 toward the city centre — Myeongdong Station is 2 stops (≈5 min, ≈₩1,350)
Text TRAVEL to 4004 to switch on Verizon TravelPass
Open KakaoMap or Naver Map — Google Maps navigation is very limited in Korea

sections.title: Local essentials
sections.body: Emergencies: 112 (police), 119 (fire & ambulance). Tap water is officially drinkable, though many locals prefer filtered or bottled. Tipping isn't expected. For maps use KakaoMap or Naver Map; for taxis, Kakao T; for translation, Papago. A little Korean goes far: annyeonghaseyo (hello), gamsahamnida (thank you).

sections.title: Entry & documents
sections.body: ⚠️ The entry rules below assume a US passport. On another passport, check your own requirements — they differ.
checklist: No visa for tourism up to 90 days
K-ETA is waived for US citizens through Dec 31, 2026 — you don't need it
Without a K-ETA you must complete the free e-Arrival Card within 3 days before arrival at the official site (e-arrivalcard.go.kr) — ignore lookalike sites that charge a fee
Optional: you may still apply for a K-ETA (₩10,000, ~$8, valid 3 years) to skip the arrival card
Passport valid for the length of your stay
Carry your MSI ticket confirmations and a power adapter (Korea uses Type C/F, 220V)

sections.title: Booking checklist
sections.body: Roughly in this order — the first group is what sells out or pins your dates.
checklist: Flights, round-trip (book early) — ≈ $1,000–1,400
Seoul Airbnb in Myeongdong for the week (booked) — ≈ $90/night
Daejeon I-Hotel — 2 nights (Jul 11 & 12), a 2-min walk to DCC II (booked) — ≈ $160
MSI tickets loaded in the NOL World app — mobile-only entry, no screenshots or prints (book early)
KTX Seoul→Daejeon (Jul 11 afternoon) and Daejeon→Seoul (Jul 13 morning) — book both ways early (Korail app) — ≈ $32 return
Changdeokgung Secret Garden timed tour (reserve ahead)
e-Arrival Card — within 72 hours of your flight
Confirm Verizon TravelPass; download KakaoMap, Naver Map, Papago, Kakao T, Korail
Break in your shoes / insoles (week before)

sections.title: Pre-flight countdown — specific actions by date
sections.body:
Time-sensitive. Do these in order — the e-Arrival Card window opens exactly 72 h before landing; do not complete it earlier or it expires.

Now — before Jul 7
Book the KTX Seoul→Daejeon (Jul 11, afternoon departure ≈15:00–15:30 — you raid GO Fest in Seoul that morning, then leave for the 17:00 match) and Daejeon→Seoul return (Jul 13, morning — you have a second Daejeon night, so no Sunday-evening rush) at korail.com or the KorailTalk app — Finals weekend trains fill fast. Your Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil, Yuseong-gu) is booked for both nights (Jul 11 & 12) — it sits ~130 m / a 2-minute walk from DCC II, so you drop your bags and stroll to both matches. Book the Changdeokgung Secret Garden timed tour for Tue Jul 14 at ticket.changdeokgung.go.kr. Install the NOL World app and confirm MSI tickets open (mobile-only — no screenshots, no prints).

Jul 5 (Sun) — 3 days before
Start the jet lag clock: go to bed 1 hour earlier than normal tonight, wake 1 hour earlier. Get 15–20 min of bright outdoor light within 30 min of waking. Dim screens after 20:00. Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin ~5 h before your new (earlier) bedtime. Stock Pokémon GO: Max Revives, Ultra Balls, Golden Razz, Silver Pinaps, Charged TMs. Build and save raid teams for Mega Mewtwo X (Flying/Fairy) and Y (Ghost/Dark).

Jul 6 (Mon) — 2 days before
Jet lag: go to bed 2 hours earlier. Same morning light + evening dim + melatonin protocol. Clear Pokémon storage to 400+ (transfer/release before the trip).

Jul 6–7 — within 3 days of landing
Complete the e-Arrival Card at e-arrivalcard.go.kr (free, 5 min). The site accepts submissions from ~3 days (72 h) before arrival — for your ~05:30 KST Jul 9 landing that window opens around Jul 6; don't try earlier or it won't be accepted, and don't leave it to the airport. Save the QR code to your camera roll offline. Jet lag: bedtime 3 hours earlier if possible. No caffeine after 12:00. Charge all battery packs and GO Plus/Gotcha. Pack: flip-flops (for jimjilbang), compact umbrella, quick-dry layers, insoles.

Jul 8 (Wed) — departure night
Depart Newark Liberty Airport (EWR) — not JFK. Air Premia flight YP132. K-ETA is waived for US citizens through Dec 31 2026 (no action needed). Check your booking confirmation for the EWR terminal. Check-in opens ~6 h before departure (≈19:30 Jul 7 EDT). Arrive at EWR by 23:00 at the latest for a smooth international process.
checklist: KTX Jul 11 Seoul → Daejeon (afternoon, ≈15:00–15:30) booked — korail.com or KorailTalk
KTX Jul 13 Daejeon → Seoul (morning return) booked
Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil) — 2 nights (Jul 11 & 12) booked; 2-min walk to DCC II
Changdeokgung Secret Garden tour Jul 14 reserved
e-Arrival Card completed at e-arrivalcard.go.kr — QR saved offline
MSI tickets loaded and tested in NOL World app (no screenshots)
GO Fest teams pre-built; Pokémon storage cleared to 400+
Departure airport confirmed: Newark Liberty EWR — not JFK

sections.title: Jet lag protocol — EWR to Seoul (13-hour eastward shift)
sections.body:
A 13-hour eastward shift is near worst-case for jet lag. Natural adaptation: ~1 hour per day = 13 days to fully adjust. With this protocol: 4–5 days. Three tools: timed light exposure, melatonin, and sleep scheduling. Sources: Frontiers in Physiology (2019), PMC1249488/PMC1262683, CDC Yellow Book Travel Health, Huberman Lab.

Pre-flight (Jul 5–7): Shift bedtime 1 hour earlier each night (see Pre-flight countdown above). Each morning get bright outdoor light within 15–30 min of waking — this is the strongest phase-advance signal available. Dim screens after 20:00 each evening (blocks the delay signals fighting your advance). Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin approximately 5 hours before your shifted bedtime — timing is critical; at this point melatonin produces maximum clock advance. Taking it too late works against you.

On the flight (01:30+ EDT Jul 8): Sleep is the goal. Eye mask and earplugs on immediately. Keep window shade closed — the polar route crosses near-continuous daylight at altitude. No alcohol: it suppresses REM and fragments sleep. No caffeine from boarding onward. Drink ~250 ml water per hour (cabin humidity is under 20%). Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin shortly after takeoff (~02:00 EDT / ~15:00 KST). Your body still reads this as ~2 a.m., so the priority on this leg is simply to sleep the overnight flight — melatonin helps you do that. (The real Seoul re-anchoring happens with morning light after you land.) When breakfast service begins near landing, eat it: meal timing helps shift your metabolic clock to Seoul rhythms.

Day 1 in Seoul (Jul 9 landing): Your internal clock says ~17:00 EDT (early evening New York). Do not nap on arrival — a full sleep cycle now locks you to NY time for days. Get outside within 1 hour of reaching your room: even 15–20 min of outdoor morning light (07:00–10:00 KST) is your strongest circadian anchor. Caffeine: use it in the morning and early afternoon only — hard cutoff at 14:00 KST. Stay active; don't sit still. Avoid bright outdoor light 17:00–19:00 KST for the first 2 days — on your NY body clock this is just-after-sunrise, and light here delays rather than advances your clock. Jimjilbang visit 17:00–21:00 (see Day 1 itinerary): the heat-then-cooling cycle triggers the temperature drop that signals sleep onset — one of the best jet-lag recovery tools available. Target bedtime: 22:00–22:30 KST. Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin at 21:00 KST. Repeat nightly for 4–5 nights.
checklist: Jul 5: Bedtime 1h earlier, morning outdoor light within 30 min of waking, dim evenings, melatonin 5h before new bedtime
Jul 6: Bedtime 2h earlier — same protocol
Jul 7: Bedtime 3h earlier; no caffeine after 12:00
On flight: eye mask on, no alcohol, water every hour, melatonin at ~02:00 EDT, eat Seoul-time breakfast near landing
Jul 9 AM: Outside for light by 09:00 KST — no nap over 30 min, caffeine cut off at 14:00 KST
Jul 9 PM: Jimjilbang 17:00–21:00 KST, melatonin 0.5–1 mg at 21:00, bed by 22:30
Jul 10–13: Morning outdoor light daily; avoid light 17:00–19:00 KST first 2 days; melatonin 21:00 nightly

sections.title: Phone & data
sections.body: Verizon TravelPass is $12/line/day and works in Korea — text TRAVEL to 4004 to enable it; a local eSIM/SIM is a cheaper alternative if you can use one. The apps that actually matter here: KakaoMap or Naver Map for directions (Google Maps routing is limited), Kakao T for taxis, Papago for translation, and Korail for KTX tickets.

sections.title: Weather & comfort
sections.body: Early-to-mid July is Korea's monsoon season (jangma) — hot, very humid, with heavy rain spells. Pack a compact umbrella and quick-dry clothes, hydrate hard, and build in air-conditioned breaks (malls, cafés, museums). For heavier feet and heel pain, use a firm, structured insole with a deep heel cup and a weight rating (Powerstep Pinnacle Maxx, Tread Labs Pace, Superfeet, or VALSOLE / Walkomfy), plus moisture-wicking socks. Break them in before the trip.

sections.title: Holidays & your dates
sections.body:
Timing is in your favour: no public holidays fall during 8–15 July 2026. Constitution Day (17 July) isn't a day off and lands just after you leave, and the next real holiday is Liberation Day on 15 August — so nothing shuts down while you're there.

What you do need to plan around is the weather. Mid-July is the peak of Korea's monsoon, 'jangma' — the wettest, most humid stretch of the year, with roughly 300–400 mm of rain across about 16 days, real heat, and humidity near 80%. The rain often arrives in heavy bursts that can briefly flood streets or slow transit, with a small typhoon chance later in the month. Pack a compact umbrella and quick-dry layers, keep indoor options in your back pocket, and check the forecast each morning. Your MSI weekend in Daejeon (11–12 July) is indoors, so rain won't touch it. (See Weather & comfort for what to wear.)

sections.title: Money & currency
sections.body:
Korea uses the won (₩). The won is weak right now, which helps your spending power — roughly (approximate, check before you go): ₩1,000 ≈ $0.67 and ₩10,000 ≈ $6.70, at about ₩1,500 to the dollar.

Cards and contactless work almost everywhere, but keep some cash for market stalls and small eateries. A T-money card covers subway and buses. Tipping isn't expected.

sections.title: Budget & daily costs
sections.intro: Rough per-person estimates in USD — Korea is good value, and the weak won (about ₩1,500 to the dollar) stretches your money further. Tap a field to log what you actually spend; the running total updates live and saves on this device. Treat the estimates as starting points and confirm as you book.

items.label: Lodging — Myeongdong Airbnb, per night (per room)
items.note: Split across the room — good value with three of you. Myeongdong is central and Line 4 connects everything. You keep it through the 2 Daejeon nights too (so those nights overlap with the Daejeon hotel cost) — the upside is you leave most of your luggage in Seoul and travel light to MSI.

items.label: Food & coffee, per day
items.note: Street food and casual spots are cheap; chain coffee ~$1.50.

items.label: Local transport, per day
items.note: Subway rides are under ~$1.10 on a T-money card.

items.label: Sights & entries, per day
items.note: Palaces are a few dollars; lots is free.

items.label: MSI weekend tickets (already booked)
items.note: You've got the LBF + Grand Final — log what you paid.

items.label: KTX Seoul↔Daejeon — return
items.note: ₩18,000–32,000 each way, ~50 min; book on the Korail app.

items.label: Daejeon I-Hotel — 2 nights (Jul 11 & 12)
items.note: Booked — 27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil, a 2-min walk from DCC II. Log what you actually paid; the second night means no Sunday-evening dash back to Seoul.

sections.title: Health & pharmacy
sections.body:
Emergencies: dial 119 for an ambulance or fire — the ambulance is free — and 112 for police. On a 119 call, say 'English, please' to be connected to an interpreter; your location is detected automatically. The number worth saving first is 1330, the 24/7 Korea Travel Hotline: English-speaking operators who interpret, give directions, and can patch you through to 119 or 112. 1339 is a 24-hour medical line that finds the nearest emergency room.

Pharmacies (약국, yakguk) show a '약' sign and are well stocked; many close on Sundays, but those by major stations stay open and some run 24 hours. For minor needs, convenience stores (CU, GS25, 7-Eleven) carry basics like paracetamol and antacids. Korea doesn't require insurance for visitors, so you'd pay the full bill upfront — bring travel insurance. Seoul tap water (Arisu) is treated and safe to drink, though locals often prefer bottled or filtered.
checklist: Save 119 (ambulance/fire), 112 (police), and 1330 (24/7 English travel hotline)
Buy travel insurance — without it you pay 100% upfront
Pack medicines in original packaging, plus a note of their generic names
Learn the sign: 약 = pharmacy; convenience stores cover the basics after hours

sections.title: Etiquette & language
sections.body:
Korean is the language; signs, the subway, and major sights are bilingual. For translation, use Papago — it's far more accurate than Google Translate for menus and signs. Google Maps barely works for directions in Korea — use KakaoMap or Naver Map.

  • Bowing: a slight nod works as hello, thank you, or goodbye — don't overthink it.
  • Two-handed exchange: give and receive cards, cash, or items with both hands (or right hand with the left touching your forearm) — it signals respect.
  • No tipping, anywhere. Genuinely none — attempting it can create awkwardness. The price on the menu is what you pay.
  • Chopstick etiquette: don't stand them upright in rice (funeral association); rest them on the holder when you're not using them.
  • Pouring drinks: pour for others at the table; let them pour for you. Don't self-pour at a shared meal.
  • Shoes off: watch for a raised floor at traditional floor-seating restaurants — it's obvious when you see it.
  • Volume: keep it quiet indoors, especially on transit.

Key phrases (Hangul + romanisation):

  • 안녕하세요 — annyeonghaseyo — hello
  • 감사합니다 — gamsahamnida — thank you (formal)
  • 얼마예요? — eolmayeyo? — how much?
  • 이거 주세요 — igeo juseyo — this one, please
  • 화장실 어디예요? — hwajangsil eodiyeyo? — where is the bathroom?
  • 괜찮아요 — gwaenchanayo — it's okay / no problem

sections.title: Key transit routes
steps: Incheon → Seoul: Air Premia arrives at Terminal 1 — the AREX station is inside T1. Take the AREX All-Stop to Seoul Station (≈59 min, ≈₩5,350) — practical choice at 06:00 KST; the Express (≈43 min, ≈₩13,000) requires a reservation and isn't worth the premium at that hour. At Seoul Station, board Line 4 — Myeongdong is 2 stops (≈5 min). Total airport → door: ~70 min.

Around Seoul from Myeongdong (Line 4): subway signed in English — tap T-money in and out; use Kakao T for taxis. Line 4 reaches most of the city without a transfer. Key hops: Gwangjang Market → change to Line 1 at Dongdaemun H&C Park (~10 min), Yongsan / HYBE / LoL Park (~5–10 min south on Line 4), Hongdae → Seoul Station then Line 2 (~20 min). SPAREX Dongdaemun jimjilbang → 2 stops to Dongdaemun H&C Park Station, Exit 14 (~8 min).

Seoul → Daejeon (Jul 11): KTX from Seoul Station (not Suseo/SRT), ~50 min — book on the Korail app or korail.com. Take a train in the 15:00–15:30 KST window on Jul 11 to reach Daejeon by ≈16:10 before the 17:00 LBF.

Daejeon Station → your hotel / DCC II: a taxi is the easy call — ~20 min, ≈₩11,000 (≈₩3,700 each, split three ways). Your Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil) is ~130 m — a 2-minute walk — from the arena, so drop bags and walk in. Cheaper backup: Metro Line 1 to Government Complex Daejeon (정부청사역, ~15 min) then a short taxi (<10 min, ≈₩6,500). Skip the direct bus 707 — it takes over an hour. DCC II: 87 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu.

Back to Seoul (Jul 13): KTX from Daejeon Station, ~50 min — you stay a second night in Daejeon, so you ride back Monday morning, not Sunday night. Book the morning return in advance anyway (post-Finals trains are busy).
Seoul → Incheon (Jul 15): AREX All-Stop from Myeongdong (2 stops to Seoul Station then AREX T1); allow ~3 hours before an international flight.

items.title: Flight — depart Newark (EWR) at 01:30 EDT
items.pace: Travel day · depart EWR 01:30 EDT · ~15 hrs in the air · land ICN Terminal 1 ~05:30–06:30 KST Jul 9
items.note: The jet lag protocol in the Plan section covers exactly what to do on this flight. Follow it.
items.body:
A night flight across the Pacific — the trip starts in the dark and ends with a Korean sunrise.

Air Premia flight YP132 departs Newark Liberty (EWR) at 01:30 EDT. The flight is approximately 14–15 hours; you land at ICN Terminal 1 at approximately 05:30–06:30 KST on Thursday Jul 9.

On the plane: Eye mask and earplugs as soon as you're settled. No alcohol. Drink water every hour (~250 ml). Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin at approximately 02:00 EDT (~15:00 KST) — your body still reads it as ~2 a.m., so it helps you sleep, which is the priority on this leg. Sleep as much as possible; keep window shades closed (the polar route has near-continuous daylight). When breakfast service comes near landing (Seoul morning KST), eat it.

Airport logistics: Your e-Arrival Card QR is saved offline (done Jul 7). On landing: follow signs to immigration, scan QR at the automated gate, pick up a T-money card in arrivals, and take the AREX All-Stop to Seoul Station (~59 min) then Line 4 two stops to Myeongdong (~5 min). Full steps in the 'When you land' checklist.
items.fit: Compression socks; something easy to sleep in on the plane.
checklist: e-Arrival Card QR saved offline (done Jul 7)
Melatonin packed in carry-on (0.5–1 mg for ~02:00 EDT)
Battery pack and all devices fully charged
Eye mask and earplugs in carry-on
Depart EWR — not JFK

items.title: Arrive Seoul — light, bathhouse, early night
items.pace: Arrival day · ICN T1 → Myeongdong ≈70 min · outdoor light by 09:00 · jimjilbang 17:00–21:00 · bed by 22:30
items.note: The most important day of the trip for jet lag. Follow the protocol exactly — especially the bedtime.
items.body:
After 15 hours in the air, Seoul delivers exactly what exhausted travellers need: a city that works perfectly at 6 a.m.

Land at ICN Terminal 1 (~05:30–06:30 KST). Scan e-Arrival Card QR at immigration. Pick up T-money card. AREX All-Stop to Seoul Station (≈59 min), then Line 4 two stops to Myeongdong (≈5 min). Check in — early check-in is arranged. Shower, change, and do not sleep.

Jet lag: get outside within 1 hour of arrival. Walk through Myeongdong's morning market stalls (they start early), grab a coffee, anything that gets you into daylight before 10:00 KST. This 15–20 min of outdoor morning light is the single strongest signal to anchor your clock to Seoul time. Stay active through the day — don't sit still.

Afternoon (17:00–21:00): SPAREX Dongdaemun jimjilbang. From Myeongdong Station, take Line 4 two stops east to Dongdaemun History & Culture Park Station (Exit 14) — B3F of Goodmorning City building. 24-hour facility; entry ≈₩12,000–15,000 pp (verify at desk). The process: pay, receive wristband + uniform (shorts/T-shirt for the co-ed communal floor); men's bath section is separate and fully nude — shower before any pool. Rotate through heated rooms (황토방 clay room, 소금방 salt room) with cooling breaks; rest on mats in the communal area. Exit by 21:00. The heat-then-cooling cycle triggers the temperature drop that is one of the most effective signals for sleep onset — ideal jet lag timing.

⚠ Tattoo policy: Call SPAREX Dongdaemun ahead if relevant (+82-2 area, check Klook/KKday listing for the direct number). Bring flip-flops; towels provided.

Dinner: Goobne Chicken Myeongdong (2F, 7 Myeongdong 8ga-gil — 3 min from Myeongdong Station Exit 6; oven-roasted, not fried; ≈₩20,000–28,000 per chicken, verify; walk-in, no break time). Melatonin 0.5–1 mg at 21:00 KST. In bed by 22:30 KST. This is the most important sleep of the entire trip.

⚠ Road of Legends (Pokémon GO pre-event, Jul 6–10): the event is live when you land. Skip raiding today — save the energy for tomorrow and GO Fest weekend.
items.fit: Light, breathable clothes. Flip-flops in your bag for the jimjilbang.
checklist: T-money card bought + loaded at ICN arrivals
TravelPass switched on (text TRAVEL to 4004)
Do NOT nap over 30 min — jet lag protocol
Outdoor light before 10:00 KST
SPAREX Dongdaemun: arrive ≈17:00, exit by 21:00 (Dongdaemun H&C Park Station, Exit 14)
Melatonin 0.5–1 mg at 21:00 KST
In bed by 22:30 KST

items.title: Palaces & old Seoul
items.pace: Full day · Gyeongbokgung ~2 hr · Bukchon ~1.5 hr · Insadong ~1.5 hr · lots of walking
items.note: Heat and humidity are the real workload — water, shade breaks, an iced-coffee stop. Your body clock is 1–2 days adjusted at this point.
items.body:
Fifteen minutes on the subway and you're six hundred years back — grand palaces, stone lanes, and a neighbourhood that still feels lived-in.

Gyeongbokgung in the morning (time it for the changing of the guard — typically 10:00 and 14:00, but ⚠ verify the current schedule as specific ceremonies are sometimes suspended); free entry if you rent a hanbok at the gate. Then uphill into Bukchon Hanok Village — quiet voices, real residents — and wind down with tea and crafts in Insadong.

🎮 Road of Legends ends tonight (Jul 10, ~20:00 KST). Spin Gyeongbokgung and Bukchon PokéStops during your visit for any bonus items. If you want to grab a quick raid before the event ends, the Gyeongbokgung area has active gyms.
items.fit: Breathable layers; umbrella doubles for sun and sudden rain.
checklist: Gyeongbokgung open today (closed Tue)
Free entry if you rent a hanbok
Respect Bukchon's quiet hours — it's a residential neighbourhood
Road of Legends ends tonight — spin stops during the visit

items.title: GO Fest Day 1 in Seoul → KTX → MSI Lower Bracket Final
items.pace: GO Fest 10:00–15:00 Seoul · KTX ≈15:30 · DCC II via metro+bus ~40 min · LBF 17:00 KST
items.note: Leave Seoul by 15:00 to reach DCC II before the 17:00 start. That gives 5 hours of GO Fest raiding in Seoul.
items.body:
The biggest weekend begins — GO Fest raids from the morning, then KTX south to the world stage.

🎮 GO Fest Global Day 1 — Seoul window (10:00–15:00 KST):
Raids go live at 10:00 KST. You have ~5 hours before you must leave for Seoul Station.

Saturday habitats — posted LeekDuck lineup (Mega + 5★ rotate hourly within each window):
Stormfire Peaks 10:00–13:00 (Seoul) — Ice/Electric/Fire: Lugia, Ho-Oh, Articuno, Zapdos, Moltres, Raikou, Entei, Suicune (5★, all shiny). Mega Blaziken, Mega Ampharos, Mega Abomasnow.
Astral Tides 13:00–16:00 (Seoul, you leave ~15:00) — Psychic/Ghost/Water: Dialga, Palkia, Giratina (Altered & Origin), Xerneas, Yveltal, Uxie/Mesprit/Azelf, Solgaleo, Lunala (5★). Mega Alakazam, Mega Gengar, Mega Swampert.
Dragonflight Summit 16:00–19:00 (Daejeon, around the LBF) — Flying/Rock/Dragon: Kyogre, Groudon, Rayquaza, Reshiram, Zekrom, Kyurem (5★, all shiny). Mega Salamence, Mega Pidgeot, Mega Aerodactyl. Raid these near DCC II / EXPO Science Park before the 17:00 match and on breaks.

Top priority all day: Mega Mewtwo X (Super Mega, Saturday-only, global debut, shiny) — it appears in every habitat, so raid it in Seoul in the morning and near the arena in Daejeon. In GO it's Psychic/Fighting → weak to Flying and Fairy only (Mega Rayquaza/Mega Pidgeot, Shadow Moltres, Mega Gardevoir, Xerneas, Zacian). Spend 3–5 of your 9 free passes here; put the rest on Stormfire (Lugia/Ho-Oh Candy XL) and Dragonflight in Daejeon (Rayquaza, Groudon, Kyogre).

📍 15:00 KST: leave for Seoul Station. Line 4 south, 2 stops (~5 min). Take a KTX departing 15:00–15:30 toward Daejeon (≈50 min → Daejeon Station ≈16:10–16:20).

Daejeon Station → hotel → DCC II: grab a taxi (~20 min, ≈₩11,000) to the Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil) — drop your bags, then it's a 2-minute (130 m) walk to DCC II. With a ~16:10 arrival at Daejeon Station you'll be at the arena well before 17:00. (Cheaper backup: Metro Line 1 to Government Complex Daejeon + short taxi, ~25 min.)

🏟 MSI Lower Bracket Final, 17:00 KST. Arrive early for security and the merch queue. Passport on you — MSI tickets are mobile-only via the NOL World app (no screenshots, no prints, no transfers). Match is best-of-5; budget 3–5 hours.

EXPO Science Park (near DCC II) has active PokéStops and gyms — if you arrive with time before the match, you can spin and raid in the plaza.

After the match: Sungsimdang (DCC 1F, 107 Expo-ro, ≈08:00–21:00) for fried soboro bread if it's not too late, or Ossi Kalguksu Doryong (knife-cut noodles, ≈0.2 mi from DCC II) for a post-match dinner. Your I-Hotel room is a 2-minute walk away.
items.fit: Team colours, plus a layer for fierce arena air-conditioning.
checklist: KTX booked — Seoul Station depart 15:00–15:30 KST
MSI NOL World app ticket loaded on your phone
Passport on you (tied to NOL World ticketing)
9 free Raid Passes today — spin Gym discs
GO Fest priority: Mega Mewtwo X first (Saturday Super Mega only)
Battery packs fully charged before leaving the hotel

items.title: GO Fest Day 2 in Daejeon → MSI Grand Final
items.pace: GO Fest 10:00–19:00 Daejeon · Grand Final 15:00 KST · 2nd night in Daejeon (no train to catch)
items.note: Your second Daejeon night removes all the pressure — watch the full Grand Final and still raid Sunday's last habitat.
items.body:
The day the season ends — and with a second Daejeon night, you get to enjoy all of it.

🎮 GO Fest Global Day 2 — Daejeon (10:00–19:00 KST):
You're in the Expo/Yuseong district all day. The EXPO Science Park plazas and the adjacent Hanbat Arboretum are the densest PokéStop/gym clusters near DCC II — raid from your hotel area and on the walk to breakfast.

Sunday habitats — posted LeekDuck lineup:
Earthforged Domain 10:00–13:00 — Ground/Steel/Normal: Regirock, Regice, Registeel, Regieleki, Regidrago, Regigigas, Heatran, Origin Dialga, Origin Palkia, plus Tornadus / Thundurus / Landorus / Enamorus (both formes each) (5★). Mega Metagross, Mega Garchomp, Mega Audino.
Verdant Anomaly 13:00–16:00 — Poison/Bug/Grass: Deoxys (all 4 formes), Genesect (all 5 drives), Necrozma, Stakataka, Blacephalon, the Tapu quartet and the Ultra Beasts. In Korea (Asia-Pacific) the regional UB is Xurkitree — you can only catch it on this continent, so make it a must. Mega Beedrill, Mega Pinsir, Mega Sceptile. Raid here until ~14:15, then walk to the arena.
Twilight Battlefield 16:00–19:00 — Dark/Fairy/Fighting: Latias, Latios, Cresselia, Darkrai, Cobalion, Terrakion, Virizion, Zacian, Zamazenta (5★, all shiny). Mega Tyranitar, Mega Gardevoir, Mega Lucario. Overlaps the Grand Final — raid on breaks with arena signal, then finish the window after the match (you're staying the night).

Top priority: Mega Mewtwo Y (Super Mega, Sunday-only, global debut, shiny) — pure Psychic, weak to Bug/Ghost/Dark; in every habitat. Then Xurkitree (Korea-only UB) in Verdant before the 15:00 match.
Secondary: Therian Landorus/Thundurus & Heatran (Earthforged); Latios & Darkrai (Twilight); Necrozma for Dusk Mane/Dawn Wings fusion.

✅ No more conflict: last year's worry — "GO Fest ends before the match does" — no longer applies. With the second night here, the only thing after the Grand Final is dinner. (Shadow Palkia in Shadow Raids runs through Aug 4 regardless, so nothing time-critical is lost.)

🏟 MSI Grand Final, 15:00 KST. The arena is a 2-minute walk from your I-Hotel room — head over by 14:15. Best-of-5; likely ends 18:00–20:30. NOL World mobile ticket required; passport on you.

Celebrate in Daejeon — no train to catch. For a night out, Dunsan-dong (Seo-gu, ~15–20 min by taxi or Metro Line 1) is Daejeon's dining-and-nightlife district — galbi, chimaek and bars; Sinsuwon Galbi (2F Gukje Bldg, 1007 Dunsan2-dong ⚠ verify hours) is a well-known grill. Closer to the hotel, Cham-i-mat Gamjatang (≈0.2 mi from DCC, pork-bone stew ⚠ hours) is ideal recovery food after a long day. Then back to your Daejeon room.
items.fit: Team colours; a layer for arena AC.
checklist: Stay relaxed — second night in Daejeon, no Sunday-night train
GO Fest priority: Mega Mewtwo Y first (Sunday Super Mega only), then Xurkitree
Raid Verdant Anomaly until ~14:15, then arena by 14:15
Twilight Battlefield (Darkrai/Zacian) 16:00–19:00 — raid around the match
Phones and battery packs charged the night before

items.title: KTX back to Seoul → Namsan, Gwangjang & Dongdaemun
items.pace: Half travel day · morning KTX Daejeon→Seoul ~50 min · Gwangjang lunch · N Seoul Tower at dusk · DDP evening
items.note: A deliberately lighter day to recover from the GO Fest/MSI weekend — ride back relaxed, then take the Seoul afternoon at your own pace.
items.body:
Ride back to Seoul at an easy pace — the hard-charging weekend is behind you. Today is meant to be light.

Morning: relaxed checkout in Daejeon, then your booked morning KTX to Seoul (~50 min). From Seoul Station, Line 4 two stops to Myeongdong; drop your bags. If the weekend wiped you out, a short rest now is completely fine — the rest of the day is flexible.

Lunch: Gwangjang Market (광장시장) for bindaetteok and mayak gimbap — quieter on a Monday than the weekend. From Myeongdong: Line 4 → Dongdaemun H&C Park → Line 1 one stop to Jongno-5-ga (Exit 7–8), ≈10 min. Cash only at most stalls.

Late afternoon → evening: N Seoul Tower on Namsan for the city view — ride the cable car or bus up (don't hike in July heat); it's best timed for dusk. Then Dongdaemun Design Plaza (DDP) after dark, when the building and the LED-rose field are lit; the surrounding Dongdaemun streets shop late if you want a final sweep.

(Changdeokgung is closed Mondays — it's tomorrow's plan.)
items.fit: Light clothes; layers for AC-heavy interiors.
checklist: Morning KTX Daejeon → Seoul (booked)
Drop bags at the Myeongdong Airbnb — a short rest is fine
Cash for Gwangjang market stalls
Cable car or bus up Namsan — N Seoul Tower at dusk
DDP after dark; Changdeokgung saved for tomorrow

items.title: The Secret Garden — then one last sweep of the city
items.pace: ~5 hr · Changdeokgung + Secret Garden ~2 hr · free afternoon for shopping
items.note: The Secret Garden is a timed guided walk — book ahead and wear real shoes for uneven paths.
items.body:
The palace garden you saved for last, then a slow final wander through streets that are starting to feel familiar.

Changdeokgung and its Secret Garden in the morning — it was closed Monday, so today is the slot. (Gyeongbokgung would be the alternative but it's shut on Tuesdays too, so this is the only palace day left anyway.) The guided walk through the rear garden is genuinely one of the best things in Seoul. Afternoon is yours: Myeongdong cosmetics, Yongsan electronics, anime at Animate, or simply walk.
items.fit: Walking shoes for the garden paths.
checklist: Secret Garden tour pre-booked
Last-minute souvenirs
Pack and weigh your bags

items.title: Fly home
items.pace: Departure · Seoul → ICN ~60–90 min · be there ~3 hr before an international flight
items.note: Give Incheon plenty of buffer — it's big and busy.
items.body: AREX or limousine bus back to Incheon for the flight home.
checklist: AREX / limousine to ICN
Tax-refund kiosk if you shopped
Passport + boarding pass

items.name: Gyeongbokgung
items.body: The largest of the Joseon palaces, with the changing-of-the-guard ceremony and the banquet hall over a lotus pond. Summer hours run to about 18:30 (last entry 17:30); closed Tuesdays, and free if you're wearing a hanbok.

img.file: Gyeonghoeru (Royal Banquet Hall) at Gyeongbokgung Palace, Seoul.jpg

items.name: Bukchon Hanok Village
items.body: Lanes of restored hanok houses on the hillside between the palaces, framed against the modern skyline. It's a living neighbourhood — keep noise down and don't peer into homes.

img.file: Bukchon-ro 11-gil street with hanok houses and blue sky in Bukchon Hanok Village Seoul.jpg

items.name: N Seoul Tower (Namsan)
items.body: On the wooded peak of Namsan, with the best wraparound view of the city. Ride the cable car or a bus up rather than hiking in the heat; the observation deck is worth timing for sunset.

items.name: Dongdaemun Design Plaza
items.body: Zaha Hadid's vast silver, curving 'spaceship', best after dark when it's lit and the LED-rose field is on. Design shops, exhibitions and the surrounding night markets make it an easy evening.

img.file: Dongdaemun Design Plaza at night, Seoul, Korea.jpg

items.name: Cheonggyecheon
items.body: A restored stream cutting through the centre with walkways below street level — a cool, easy stroll and a good flat break between sights.

items.name: Insadong
items.body: A walkable lane of galleries, traditional craft shops and tea houses, a short hop from the palaces — a natural pairing with a Gyeongbokgung morning.

sections.title: Visiting tips
sections.body: Time the palaces around their closed days — Gyeongbokgung is shut Tuesdays, Changdeokgung Mondays — and pre-book the Changdeokgung Secret Garden, which only enters on timed guided walks. Bukchon is residential, so it's best early and quiet. And don't fight the July heat: treat the air-conditioned stops (DDP, COEX, museums) as the spine of the hottest afternoons.

sections.title: Your MSI weekend
sections.body: The Mid-Season Invitational runs at the Daejeon Convention Center II (Hall 2), the first international League event ever held in Daejeon. Your tickets cover the Lower Bracket Final on Sat Jul 11 and the Grand Final on Sun Jul 12 — the climax of the tournament. The hall holds roughly 8,000, so arrive early for security and the merch line, and have your passport handy since it's tied to ticketing. Your Daejeon I-Hotel is ~130 m from the hall — a 2-minute walk — so both days you simply stroll over.

sections.title: Getting to the arena
sections.body: From Seoul Station, the KTX reaches Daejeon in about 50 minutes (book on the Korail app or korail.com; use Seoul Station, not Suseo/SRT). From Daejeon Station, a taxi to the arena is ~20 min, ≈₩11,000 (split three ways it's pocket change); the cheaper metro-plus-taxi combo runs ~25 min via Government Complex Daejeon (정부청사역). The arena is Daejeon Convention Center II (DCC II), 87 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu — and your Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil) is ~130 m from it, a 2-minute walk, so you stroll to both matches and skip the post-match scramble entirely. While you're there, Sungsimdang (DCC 1F, 107 Expo-ro) is Daejeon's legendary bakery — fried soboro and chive breads; queue moves fast. EXPO Science Park is adjacent to DCC II with open plazas, PokéStops, and gyms — useful for GO Fest raiding windows between matches.

sections.title: Hongdae after dark
sections.body: The university district around Hongik is the city's youth-and-nightlife core — street buskers, arcades and claw-machine halls, board-game and themed cafés, late-night eats, and anime goods (look for Animate). It's the natural evening base for the group when you're not at the arena.

sections.title: PC bangs, arcades & LoL Park
sections.body: Korea's PC bangs — high-spec gaming cafés charging by the hour, often with food service — are everywhere and cheap; they're a rite of passage. Arcades lean into rhythm and crane games. League fans should check the LCK schedule: LoL Park in Jongno is the league's home studio/arena, and the Summer split resumes after MSI, so a live LCK match in Seoul may be on while you're there.

sections.title: Anime & game shopping
sections.body:
Animate Seoul (5F, AK& Mall, 188 Yanghwa-ro, Mapo-gu — Hongdae Station Exit 9; open ~11:00–22:00) is the largest anime specialist in the Hongdae area: figures, manga, Blu-ray, merchandise, and rotating pop-ups for seasonal titles. Yongsan i'Park Mall electronics floors carry games, trading cards, and figures — verify the current tenant mix at the mall, as renovations have shifted some vendors. SMTOWN COEX Artium (Gangnam; open 10:30–22:00) is the K-pop one-stop: SM artists' albums, photocards, limited merch, and a café. Myeongdong K-pop street around Myeongdong Station has comprehensive stock across all agencies; go early or skip if the weekend crowds are too much.

HYBE Insight (42 Hangang-daero, Yongsan-gu; BTS/HYBE fan museum) requires timed-entry tickets booked weeks in advance via the official app or site — book the moment you confirm your trip dates. No walk-in entry; no late arrivals. LoL Park (3F Gran Seoul Building, 33 Jong-ro, Jongno-gu; open 11:00–20:00; Jonggak Station Exit 1) is the LCK's home venue with a free-to-browse exhibition floor, Riot merch store, and PC café. LCK Summer split resumes after MSI — check the schedule for a live match while you're there.

sections.title: What to eat
sections.body:
Hit the obvious greats: Korean BBQ, chimaek (fried chicken and beer), tteokbokki, and market street food at Gwangjang — bindaetteok (mung-bean pancake), mayak gimbap, and live octopus if you're brave.

Your Myeongdong base is the tourist-heavy K-beauty strip — the stalls are legitimately good value: egg bread, hotteok, giant grilled squid, and tteokbokki carts every evening. It's fine to eat there without embarrassment. For less-touristy meals: Gwangjang Market (~10 min, Line 4 → Line 1) for the real market experience, Mapo/Hongdae area for Korean BBQ (~20 min, Line 4 → Line 2), and Yongsan for beef and grills (~5 min south on Line 4). In July heat, naengmyeon (cold buckwheat noodles) and bingsu (shaved-ice dessert) are exactly what you want. Café culture is everywhere when you need air-conditioning.

sections.title: Where to eat — picks near your route
sections.body:
All anchored to Myeongdong station (Line 4). All prices ≈ — verify before you go.

Myeongdong street stalls — along Myeongdong 8-gil (the main pedestrian drag outside your door). Any evening, any night of the trip. No booking, no transit. Egg bread, tteokbokki, grilled skewers, hotteok — ≈₩2,000–5,000 per item.

Goobne Chicken Myeongdong (sit-down, oven-roasted chicken) — 2F, 7 Myeongdong 8ga-gil, Jung-gu. 3 min from Myeongdong Station Exit 6 — no transit. Open all day, no break time. ≈₩20,000–28,000 per chicken (≈ — verify). Walk-in. Oven-roasted (lighter than fried); the Gochu Basasak (spicy) is the signature. Ideal arrival-night dinner (Thu Jul 9) — close, easy, no reservation.

Gwangjang Market (광장시장) — 88 Changgyeonggung-ro, Jongno-gu. From Myeongdong: Line 4 two stops to Dongdaemun H&C Park, transfer to Line 1, one stop to Jongno-5-ga, Exit 7–8 (~10 min total). Food vendors open from 09:00; closed Sundays. Cash only at most stalls. Bindaetteok ≈₩4,000, mayak gimbap ≈₩3,000. Best on Day 7 (Mon Jul 13) — weekday is emptier. Walk-in only.

AYCE Korean BBQ — budget pork belly
Ungteori Meats (엉터리생고기) — 118 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. From Myeongdong: Line 4 to Seoul Station, transfer to Line 2, Hongik Univ. Exit 3 (~20 min). Open 11:00–23:00 daily. ≈₩14,800 pp; confirm on Creatrip. Fresh (not frozen) samgyeopsal. Walk-in; may queue on weekend evenings. Best on Day 3 (Fri Jul 10) after an afternoon in Hongdae.

AYCE Korean BBQ — mid-range / beef option
MUSSO Hongdae (무쏘 홍대점) — 10 Jandari-ro, Mapo-gu. From Myeongdong: Line 4 → Seoul Station → Line 2 → Hongik Univ. (~20 min). Open 24 hours. ≈₩20,800–23,800 pp (beef, shrimp, yukhoe at higher tier). Walk-in. Good for late nights after returning from MSI.

Chimaek (fried chicken + beer)
Chicken Baengi (치킨뱅이 홍대점) — 127 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. From Myeongdong: Line 4 → Seoul Station → Line 2 → Hongik Univ. Exit 3 (~20 min). Mon–Thu 16:00–01:00; Fri–Sun 14:00–01:00. ≈₩10,000–20,000 pp; confirm at venue. Garlic chicken is the standout. Walk-in; busy Fri/Sat after 20:00 — arrive early. Best on Day 5 (Sun Jul 12) as the post-Grand Final celebration.

Cold noodles (July heat)
Yookssam Naengmyeon (육쌈냉면 홍대점) — 76 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. From Myeongdong: Line 4 → Seoul Station → Line 2 → Hongik Univ. Exit 9 (~22 min). Hours ⚠ not confirmed for 2026 — check yookssam.com. ≈₩15,000–18,000 pp (naengmyeon set with charcoal-grilled bulgogi). Walk-in for lunch; dinner can queue.

Bingsu
Sulbing (설빙) — multiple branches in and around Myeongdong; look for the nearest one on Kakao Map. Open ~12:00–24:00. Injeolmi bingsu ≈₩8,900; mango/melon ≈₩14,900–15,900; confirm on sulbing.com. No booking needed. Perfect mid-afternoon on any hot day.

Daejeon — near DCC II (Expo district, Yuseong-gu)
Sungsimdang DCC Branch — 107 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu, on the 1F of the convention centre itself. Open ⚠ ~08:00–21:00 — verify on site. Legendary Daejeon bakery; fried soboro and chive breads. Walk-in; grab breakfast before the arena on Sat Jul 11.
Ossi Kalguksu Doryong — ~0.2 mi from DCC II (search Kakao Map). Highly-rated knife-cut noodle soup; local favourite. Open lunch/dinner ⚠ — check before going. ≈₩9,000–12,000 pp. Walk-in. Good pre-match dinner Sat Jul 11.
Cham-i-mat Gamjatang & Sundaeguk (Expo branch) — ~0.2 mi from DCC II. Pork-bone stew (gamjatang) and blood-sausage soup — hearty recovery food after a long event day. ≈₩9,000–13,000 pp. Open ⚠ — verify. Walk-in.
Big night out (Sun Jul 12 — second Daejeon night, no train to catch): Dunsan-dong (Seo-gu, ~15–20 min by taxi or Metro Line 1) is Daejeon's dining-and-nightlife district — galbi, chimaek and bars. Sinsuwon Galbi (2F, Gukje Bldg, 1007 Dunsan2-dong, Seo-gu ⚠ verify hours) is a well-known grill there; book ahead for a group on a weekend evening.

Convenience store — the underrated late-night move
GS25 and CU everywhere, open 24 h: triangle gimbap ~₩1,500, instant ramen ~₩1,500, egg salad sandwich ~₩2,500, tteokbokki cups. After a full GO Fest / MSI day, often the right call.

sections.title: Shopping in Seoul
sections.body:
You're based in Myeongdong — K-beauty one-stop (the big Olive Young flagship is steps away) and street food. By district from there: Hongdae (~20 min, Line 4 → Line 2) for youth fashion; Gangnam / COEX (~30 min, Line 4 → Line 2 → Line 9) for malls; Dongdaemun (~5 min, Line 4 or walk) for late-night shopping; Insadong (~15 min walk or 1 stop) for traditional crafts. Tax-free shops offer a VAT refund on purchases over ₩15,000 — instant in-store or at the airport kiosk on departure.

Streetwear near Hongdae (Line 4 → Seoul Station → Line 2, ~20 min): Musinsa Standard flagship (144 Yanghwa-ro, Mapo-gu; open 11:00–21:00; Exit 9) — Korean casualwear at a wide price range. The alley cluster around Wausan-ro (Mahagrid, SOUR, indie labels) rewards a slow browse. Gentle Monster Hongdae (54 Dokmak-ro 7-gil; open 12:00–22:00) is an art-installation eyewear store — free to walk through. Ader Error has moved to Seongsu (Seongdong-gu); worth the trip if design-forward Korean fashion is your thing.

sections.title: Souvenirs — Korea
items: K-beauty from Olive Young (sheet masks, skincare)
Korean snacks (honey-butter chips, Pepero, Market O)
Kakao Friends / Line Friends character goods
A celadon or craft piece from Insadong

sections.title: Collectibles & chain merch
sections.body: For collectors: a Starbucks 'Been There' Seoul mug, and the Kakao Friends and Line Friends flagship stores (Hongdae and Gangnam) for character-brand exclusives you won't find at home.

sections.title: GO Fest Global 2026 — what to know
sections.body:
Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global runs Saturday Jul 11 and Sunday Jul 12, 10:00 a.m.–7:00 p.m. local time worldwide — not location-gated. This year's event is completely free for all trainers (no ticket purchase or payment needed). Everyone gets: Special Research leading to Zeraora (Mythical, Electric-type — first appearance in Pokémon GO; research does not expire), Branching Timed Research (choose Mega Mewtwo X or Y path), and all event bonuses below.

⚠ Re-verify in the final week. Niantic finalises exact habitat boss lists, Super Mega mechanics, and shiny rates only days before the event. Everything in this section reflects the official pre-announcement and LeekDuck's tracking as of June 2026 — re-check LeekDuck and the in-game news the week of Jul 6, and adjust your priority targets if the lineup shifts.

Bonuses active 10:00–19:00 KST both days: up to 9 free Raid Passes per day from Gym discs, boosted Shiny encounter rates (historically ~1/20 for all legendaries and Super Mega bosses), lure modules last 1 hour, Incense-exclusive spawns (Unown, Tropius, Bouffalant), Party Play active, type-themed Field Research refreshes hourly with each habitat.

Bonuses active all day both days (00:00–23:59): up to 6 Special Trades per day (normally 1), 50% reduced Stardust cost on all trades, open 50 Gifts per day.

Raid rotation — habitats cycle every 3 hours (this is the posted LeekDuck lineup; Mega + 5-star bosses rotate hourly within each window):
Sat: Stormfire Peaks 10–13 (Ice/Electric/Fire) → Astral Tides 13–16 (Psychic/Ghost/Water) → Dragonflight Summit 16–19 (Flying/Rock/Dragon)
Sun: Earthforged Domain 10–13 (Ground/Steel/Normal) → Verdant Anomaly 13–16 (Poison/Bug/Grass) → Twilight Battlefield 16–19 (Dark/Fairy/Fighting)
Super Mega all day: Mega Mewtwo X (Sat) / Mega Mewtwo Y (Sun) rotate through every habitat. Full boss lists are in the day-by-day entries and counter charts below.

Mega Mewtwo debut (Super Mega tier): Mega Mewtwo X on Saturday, Mega Mewtwo Y on Sunday — first appearances in Pokémon GO. Both have shiny available. Mewtwo caught from Mega X raids knows Counter (fast); Mewtwo from Mega Y raids knows Psystrike (charged). Build teams in advance — see counter charts.

Shadow Palkia is in Shadow Raids every weekend Jul 1–Aug 4 (including GO Fest weekend). Shiny available.

Road of Legends pre-event (Jul 6–10): You land Jul 9 and catch the last 2 days of this pre-event. Features Pokémon Background collectibles from raids and GO Pass rewards (Lucky Trinkets, Gold Bottle Caps, bonus XP/candy). Spin stops on Jul 9 arrival day and Jul 10 palaces day to collect.

Ultra Unlock (from Jul 13): Niantic confirmed post-GO Fest Ultra Unlock content starting Jul 13, details to be announced after the global challenge is complete during the event weekend. Check pokemongo.com after Jul 12.

  • Raid pass strategy: 9 free passes per day = 18 raids across the weekend without spending PokéCoins. Saturday: use in Seoul (10:00–15:00 KST) before the KTX. Sunday: use in Daejeon all day (10:00–19:00 KST) — morning around EXPO Science Park / Hanbat Arboretum before the Grand Final, then Twilight Battlefield around and after the match (you stay a second night, so no early exit).
  • Remote passes: Save for Jul 9–10 and Jul 13–14 Seoul days when you're not at the arena.

sections.title: Already did Copenhagen? Your Global divergence
sections.body:
You raided GO Fest 2026 Copenhagen in person (Fælledparken, Jun 12–14), so much of the Global headline content is a repeat for you. Here's what changes when you raid the Global weekend in Korea.

✅ Already done at Copenhagen — deprioritise at Global:

  • Zeraora — you earned it from the Copenhagen Special Research. Global runs the same Zeraora line; if you completed it there you won't receive a second one. Nothing to chase.
  • Mega Mewtwo X & Y — both appeared in Copenhagen's 1,000-trainer Super Mega finale, and your in-person Mewtwo learned both Counter and Psystrike (better than the single-move Mewtwos from Global X or Y raids). At Global they're only worth a pass if you're still missing the shiny or want more Mega Energy / XL candy — they drop from must-do to an optional shiny re-hunt.
  • Entei, Moltres (Stormfire, Sat) and Kyogre, Groudon (Dragonflight, Sat) — caught at Copenhagen, where you also got the Primal Kyogre/Groudon that outclass the Global 5-star forms. Re-raid only for a missing shiny or as raw counters (Groudon/Kyogre still counter Xurkitree).
  • Shiny Wash Rotom — an in-person Copenhagen exclusive, not in the Global event. Nothing to do here.

🔼 Fresh at Global — your real priorities now:

  • S — Xurkitree (Sun Verdant 13–16, Daejeon): the Asia-Pacific regional Ultra Beast, not in the Copenhagen pool and catchable only on this continent. The single most valuable target of your Global weekend.
  • The legendaries Copenhagen lacked (its 5-star pool was only Moltres/Mewtwo/Entei): Rayquaza, Reshiram/Zekrom/Kyurem (Sat Dragonflight); Giratina Origin, Dialga/Palkia, lake trio, Xerneas/Yveltal, Solgaleo/Lunala (Sat Astral); Therian Landorus/Thundurus, Heatran, Origin Dialga/Palkia, the Regi trio (Sun Earthforged); Latias/Latios, Cresselia, Darkrai, Cobalion/Terrakion/Virizion, Zacian, Zamazenta (Sun Twilight). Spend the bulk of your ~18 weekend passes here.
  • Incense regionals — Tropius & Bouffalant (plus all-form Unown): normally region-locked to other continents; Global incense pulls them in anywhere, so pop an Incense for easy dex fills.
  • Verdant collection (Sun 13–16): Deoxys (all 4 forms), Genesect (all 5 drives), Necrozma (fusion fodder), the Tapu quartet, Nihilego — none were at Copenhagen.

Net effect: skip Zeraora and the Mewtwos unless you want the shiny; make Xurkitree your Sunday must, and otherwise hoover up the legendaries and incense regionals Copenhagen never offered.

sections.title: Beyond raids — research, eggs & wild spawns (Global)
sections.body:
The Global weekend isn't only raids — here's the rest, and how much to care given your Copenhagen run.

  • Special Research → Zeraora: free for everyone who logs in, but it's the same line you completed in Copenhagen — likely nothing new for you (see the divergence note above).
  • Incense spawns (both days): all forms of Unown, Tropius, and Bouffalant. Tropius/Bouffalant are region-locked elsewhere, so burn an Incense each day for the Pokédex entries; lures last 1 hour during event hours — drop one near DCC II / EXPO Science Park on Sunday.
  • Wild spawns: each habitat's wild pool follows its type theme (Verdant = Poison/Bug/Grass, etc.). Niantic hadn't posted the exact Global wild list as of this writing — ⚠ check LeekDuck the week of Jul 6. Otherwise catch with an eye on the day's featured types for Stardust and XL.
  • Eggs: the Global egg pool wasn't detailed, and unlike Copenhagen (½ Hatch Distance + 1.5× Hatch Candy) the Global bonuses include no egg-distance discount — so don't burn incubators here; spend the time raiding.
  • Trades (all weekend): up to 6 Special Trades/day and 50% Stardust on every trade. With three of you, line up swaps for Xurkitree, the new legendaries, and any Copenhagen-vs-Korea dex gaps.

sections.title: Pre-GO Fest checklist
checklist: Pokémon storage to 400+ (transfer/release beforehand)
Item bag cleared: stock up on Max Revives, Ultra Balls, Golden Razz Berries
Lucky Eggs ready (use before evolving / before mass Stardust bonuses)
Star Pieces ready (use during raids and catches to maximise Stardust)
Silver Pinap Berries stocked (use on Legendaries you need Candy XL for)
Charged TMs ready — re-roll shadow Pokémon and new Mega moves on day 1
Purified Gems ready for Shadow Raids if any appear
9 free Raid Passes per day — remember to spin Gym discs, not just battle
Pokémon GO Plus / Gotcha charged and connected for auto-catches during downtime
Battery pack fully charged; portable charger in bag for the 9-hour event window
Branching Research choice made: Mega Mewtwo X (Psychic/Fighting) or Y (pure Psychic)
Teams pre-built for Mega Mewtwo X (Flying/Fairy counters) and Y (Ghost/Dark/Bug counters)

sections.title: Priority targets — which raids to spend passes on
sections.body:
⚠ Did GO Fest Copenhagen? See Your Global divergence above — it demotes Zeraora and the Mewtwos for you and promotes Xurkitree plus the legendaries Copenhagen lacked. The S/A/B tiers below are the general ranking.

9 free passes a day and a packed match schedule — spend them on debuts, the region-lock, and meta picks. Every GO Fest legendary 5★ and Super Mega is 1/20 shiny (the best odds in the game). Tiers below are tuned to your weekend: Saturday is Seoul morning then Daejeon around the LBF; Sunday is Daejeon all day around the GF.

  • S — do these no matter what:
    Mega Mewtwo X (Sat, every habitat incl. Daejeon) — global debut, best Psychic attacker; shiny. Weak to Flying/Fairy.
    Mega Mewtwo Y (Sun, every habitat) — global debut, even stronger Psychic; shiny. Weak to Ghost/Dark/Bug.
    Xurkitree (Sun Verdant 13–16, Daejeon) — Korea-only regional UB, top non-shadow Electric attacker; you can't get it at home. Raid it before the 15:00 GF. Weak to Ground.
  • A — high value if passes/time allow:
    Sat Dragonflight (Daejeon, around the LBF): Rayquaza (best F2P Dragon + Candy XL), Groudon (top Ground, and your Xurkitree counter), Kyogre (top Water), Reshiram/Zekrom (top Fire/Electric for ML), Mega Salamence.
    Sun Earthforged (morning): Therian Landorus (best Ground attacker), Therian Thundurus (top Electric), Heatran (top Fire/Steel), Origin Dialga/Palkia.
    Sun Twilight (around the GF): Latios (top Dragon), Darkrai (top Dark PvE), Zacian (top ML), Zamazenta (great GL/UL wall).
  • B — fill / collection: Giratina Origin (Sat Astral, top ML Ghost), Deoxys Attack (Sun Verdant, glass-cannon PvE), Necrozma (Sun Verdant, fusion fodder for Dusk Mane/Dawn Wings), Nihilego (Sun Verdant, GL/UL PvP), the Regi trio + Regigigas (Sun Earthforged), Tapu quartet (Sun Verdant, PvP Fairy).
  • Shadow Palkia runs in Shadow Raids all weekend (separate from the habitat rotation). Grab one if a gym is handy, but it persists through Aug 4 — not urgent.
  • Pass math: Sat — 3–5 on Mega Mewtwo X, the rest on Dragonflight in Daejeon. Sun — 3–4 on Mega Mewtwo Y, 2–3 on Xurkitree, the rest on Twilight around the GF.

Your 6-team for the debuts (lead a Mega to boost the lobby; exact moves are in the counter charts below):
Mega Mewtwo X — weak to Flying / Fairy: Mega Rayquaza (or Mega Pidgeot) · Shadow Moltres · Rayquaza · Mega Gardevoir · Xerneas · Zacian.
Mega Mewtwo Y — weak to Bug / Ghost / Dark: Mega Tyranitar (or Mega Gengar) · Dawn Wings Necrozma · Shadow Tyranitar · Shadow Hydreigon · Chandelure · Mega Absol.
No Mega of your own? A full lobby of the listed Shadow/non-Mega counters still clears either Super Mega with 6+ trainers — aim for a group, not a duo.

sections.title: Raid counter charts — GO Fest Global 2026

bosses.name: Mega Mewtwo X
bosses.shiny_note: Global debut — first appearance in Pokémon GO
bosses.trainers: 10–20 trainers (Super Mega tier — harder than standard Mega)
bosses.note: Saturday Jul 11 only — appears in every habitat, so you can raid it in Seoul in the morning and again near the arena in Daejeon. Branching Research lets you choose the X or Y encounter.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Flying and Fairy only. (In Pokémon GO, Psychic/Fighting takes neutral damage from Ghost, Dark and Bug — don't bring those.) Flying is the top damage type: Mega Rayquaza (Dragon Ascent) is best if you have it; otherwise lead Mega Pidgeot — which is in Saturday's rotation — or Mega Gardevoir. Shadow Moltres and Rayquaza head the non-Mega Flying picks; Xerneas and Zacian add strong Fairy.

bosses.name: Mega Mewtwo Y
bosses.shiny_note: Global debut — first appearance in Pokémon GO
bosses.note: Sunday Jul 12 only — appears in every habitat, so raid it morning and afternoon around the Grand Final. Pure Psychic — more counters available than Mega X.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Bug, Ghost, Dark. Ghost and Dark are the strongest damage types here. Dawn Wings Necrozma is the single best counter in the game; Mega Tyranitar and Mega Gengar round out the premium trio. Shadow Tyranitar or Shadow Hydreigon are excellent budget shadow leads.

bosses.name: Rayquaza
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF; raid near DCC II / EXPO Science Park. Top F2P Dragon attacker and Candy XL for Master League. (Mega Rayquaza is not a raid boss this event — use Mega Salamence as your Dragon Mega lead.)
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ice (2×), Dragon, Fairy, Rock. Ice hits for double super-effective — always lead Ice. Standard 5-star difficulty; 2–3 high-level trainers handle it comfortably.

bosses.name: Kyogre
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF. Top Water attacker and Candy XL; doubles as a Groudon and Xurkitree counter.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Grass, Electric. Zekrom and Shadow Raikou are the top Electric picks; Roserade or Kartana for Grass. Zekrom's Fusion Bolt is the hardest-hitting charged move here.

bosses.name: Groudon
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF. Top Ground attacker, Candy XL for Master League, and your best Xurkitree counter on Sunday.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Water, Grass, Ice. Water hits hardest — Kyogre (in the same habitat) and Mega Swampert / Mega Gyarados lead. Kartana and Mega Sceptile bring Grass; Mamoswine and Galarian Darmanitan bring Ice.

bosses.name: Giratina (Origin Forme)
bosses.note: Saturday Astral Tides habitat (1–4 PM). Top PvP pick for Master League; worth stacking Candy XL.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ghost, Ice, Dragon, Dark, Fairy. Ghost and Dragon hit hardest. Shadow Gengar with Lick + Shadow Ball is the accessible shadow lead. Dawn Wings Necrozma is premier.

bosses.name: Xurkitree
bosses.shiny_note: Korea is in the Asia-Pacific region where Xurkitree appears — region-locked Ultra Beast, you can't get it at home
bosses.note: Sunday Verdant Anomaly (1–4 PM), Daejeon — the Asia-Pacific regional Ultra Beast and a top non-shadow Electric attacker. Raid it before the 3 PM Grand Final; it's the single most location-exclusive thing on the weekend.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ground only (pure Electric) — and Ground is immune to its Electric attacks, so a full Ground team takes almost no damage. Lead Mega Garchomp or Excadrill; Groudon (from Saturday) and Therian Landorus are premium.

bosses.name: Necrozma
bosses.note: Sunday Verdant Anomaly (1–4 PM). Base Necrozma — fuse with Solgaleo for Dusk Mane or Lunala for Dawn Wings (the game's best Ghost attacker). Worth a couple of raids as fusion fodder.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Bug, Ghost, Dark (pure Psychic — same profile as Mega Mewtwo Y, so reuse that team). Mega Gengar, Dawn Wings Necrozma and Mega Tyranitar are premier; Chandelure and Shadow Tyranitar are accessible.

bosses.name: Darkrai
bosses.note: Sunday Twilight Battlefield (4–7 PM). Strong PvE attacker; worth multiple shiny hunts.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fighting, Bug, Fairy. Fighting-type attackers hit hardest. Mega Lopunny is the best Mega boost here; Lucario and Conkeldurr are accessible shadow leads.

bosses.name: Zacian (Hero of Many Battles)
bosses.note: Sunday Twilight Battlefield (4–7 PM). Top Master League meta pick; high priority.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Poison, Steel. Mega Beedrill and Nihilego are premier Poison picks; Metagross and Dialga cover Steel. Poison hits hardest — build your best Poison team.
